title: The Bar
narrativeGoal: Establish the tension between Marcus and Elena — she needs something from him, he knows it, and both are pretending this is just a drink.
readerEffect: Feel the weight of shared history and the distance that's grown in it

knows: Marcus knows more than he's saying

characterKnowledgeChanges.marcus: Learns Elena is in real danger, not just chasing a story
characterKnowledgeChanges.elena: Learns Marcus still has contacts he claims to have left behind

subtext.surfaceConversation: Two old acquaintances catching up over drinks
subtext.actualConversation: Elena testing whether Marcus can still be trusted; Marcus deciding whether to get involved
subtext.enforcementRule: Neither character may directly state that Elena needs Marcus's help or that Marcus is considering whether to give it

marcus: Uses the bar work as escape from conversation
elena: Tries journalistic technique before dropping to genuine
Uses his name as a weapon

pacing: Slow build. Long pauses. Let the bar fill the silence.
sensoryNotes: Ground every exchange in physical detail — what hands are doing, what the ice is doing, the quality of light shifting.
sceneSpecificProhibitions: No flashbacks — history emerges only through present-tense implication
No internal monologue from Elena — we're in Marcus's POV only

anchorLines.text: You ever notice how the ice never melts the same way twice?
anchorLines.placement: Early — Marcus's deflection when Elena first probes

anchorLines.text: She set down her glass the way you put down something you're done pretending to enjoy.
anchorLines.placement: Final third, the moment the pretense drops

chunkDescriptions: Arrival and setup — Elena enters, initial exchange, the pretense of casual
The conversation — probing, deflecting, the subtext thickening
The turn — pretense drops, something real surfaces, scene ends on action
failureModeToAvoid: Characters stating their emotions or motivations directly. The scene must work through behavior and dialogue subtext, never through internal explanation.
